## Why Use Meela to Hire Your Doula in Fontana?

Meela is the trusted place for Fontana parents to find top-rated doulas, connecting over 2,000 families each month with vetted professionals. Whether you’re in Southridge, Hunter’s Ridge, or North Fontana, you can connect directly with doulas or answer a few quick questions and let us match you with the best available options nearby. All for free, no strings attached.

## Certified, Expert-Vetted Doulas in Fontana

Families in Fontana can count on Meela to connect them with doulas who provide exceptional, evidence-based, and compassionate care. Every doula listed on Meela is carefully vetted by a licensed medical professional, carries active certifications, and is backed by strong client recommendations. With extensive training and real-world experience in perinatal support, our doulas are committed to guiding families through pregnancy, birth, and beyond with confidence and care.

![Certified, Expert-Vetted Doulas in Fontana](https://www.hellomeela.com/wp-content/themes/my-listing-child/assets/new/images/vetted-doulas-near-you.png)

#### About Wendy

I've been a birth worker for over 25 years and have helped over 100 families. I will support and advocate for what you need because being heard, being validated, being understood, and being autonomous in your birth is what results in positive birth outcomes. A big part of my role as a doula is educating and making sure my clients know they are the leader in their care.

##### **Care philosophy**

A birth philosophy can include choices about pain medications (from spinal block to completely unmedicated), positions you’d feel comfortable pushing in, whom you want at the birth, ways you want your partner to help you, if you want music playing, or cultural values relating to rituals with the placenta.

#### About Hannah

Hello, I'm a proud mother of four wonderful children and a military spouse. As a new birth doula currently working toward my DONA International certification, I am dedicated to providing compassionate, personalized care throughout your pregnancy journey. I offer two prenatal visits to help you create a birth plan and guide you through essential birthing exercises. During labor and delivery, I will be there to support you and your partner every step of the way. I also provide breastfeeding support to ensure a smooth start for you and your baby. Additionally, I offer one postpartum visit to help ensure a strong bond between you and your newborn.

##### **Care philosophy**

My philosophy of care is rooted in compassion, respect, and empowerment. I believe that every birthing person deserves to feel supported, informed, and confident in their journey, regardless of the path they choose. As a birth doula, my role is to provide emotional, physical, and informational support to both you and your partner, helping to create a positive, empowering birth experience.

##### **Areas served**

#### About Momo

Hello everyone, my name is Momo Bass and I am a certified birth doula as well as a trained postpartum doula that serves the greater Los Angeles and Pasadena area. Aside from doing this rewarding work for awhile, I am also a mama myself, to three amazing kiddos! When I'm not cheering on my kids at soccer practice, you'll likely find me tending to my ever-growing houseplant collection, or tending to myself at the gym. But my true passion lies in supporting families embarking on their incredible birthing journey. Motherhood is what threw me into the thralls of the what it means to bring life into this world. It's this firsthand knowledge, coupled with my relentless curiosity about the miracle of birth and the transition into becoming a parent, that led me to become a birth and postpartum doula. I am coming out of a hiatus since having my son, but have been very active within this past year.

##### **Care philosophy**

Bringing my kiddos earth side became my superpower, and it’s become my mission to ignite that same fire in the families I work with. I believe that starts with finding judgment free informational and emotional support, and I believe it starts when parents take back control of how their labor & postpartum experience goes by making informed decisions on what’s best for yourself and your family.

#### About Jillian

I am an enthusiast of humans helping humans. Although I have not experienced any of my own pregnancies, I have physically, mentally, and emotionally with my mom's three experiences. I've seen my mom go through each pregnancy differently as well as each of their births being unique in their own way but beautiful in every way. I have experienced seeing a supportive birth team by your side helps every woman feel more empowered and supported during this beautiful experience. My reason for becoming a birth doula is to come alongside each woman to support their desired birth plan.

##### **Care philosophy**

As a doula, I feel the importance of being the best calming presence I can be, I've learned that just having someone there to listen, guide, and sometimes literally carry your weight can make your birth and postpartum experience all the difference. I connect with my clients on a deeper personal level and create that relationship bond rather than client and doula but coming together as a team.

#### About Dani

I have been practicing as a full spectrum doula since 2014, beginning by training with The Doula Project in New York as a birth doula and a perinatal loss and abortion doula, and then becoming a Certified Birth and Postpartum Doula through Doula Trainings International.

I have worked with clients who speak French, Spanish, and English, and am proud to offer services in these three languages. My approach is evidence-based and my practice centers on healing and working to end systemic disparities in care. I seek to witness each person’s experience, to offer nonjudgmental support, a feminist, queer and anti-racist lens, and a grounded and adaptable presence. I am deeply grateful to the community with whom I continue to build and grow.

##### **Care philosophy**

I believe that everyone is the expert on their own body and life and deserves to be held with care through life's transitions. Welcoming a child, birthing, and becoming parents are sacred processes. I work with birthing people, their partners,+ surrogates as they embrace + remember their power to sustain through change. I support individuals + families postpartum as they grow + navigate new roles.

#### About Natasha

I found my way to the birth world after having a very long and challenging labor with my first child. Through the 3 day process, I could not imagine having done it without my doula. I want to make sure every birthing family feels empowered during their pregnancy, birth, and postpartum period. Being a Lactation Consultant I can help ensure your breastfeeding journey gets off to a great start and help you should you encounter any challenges along the way.

I'm happy to support families from preconception, pregnancy, birth, postpartum, as well as through miscarriage/loss/abortion. I love introducing women to the ancient practice of vaginal steaming so that they may prepare for labor, shorten labor, and help them heal postpartum all in a very relaxing way. I love to ensure that new families, in particular the birthing person, are well cared for as they adjust to life with a newborn.

For postpartum clients I enjoy cooking nourishing meals and being a supportive, encouraging, and knowledgeable guide. As a mother of 2 myself, I know how valuable support throughout your transition to parenthood can be. Feel free to message or email me to set up a time to Zoom or chat and see if I'm the right doula for you!

##### **Care philosophy**

I offer non-judgmental care to help you have a birth where you feel empowered and that look back on with fondness. With lots of knowledge regarding lactation, I can help ensure your baby feeding journey gets off to an excellent start. I am also a Placenta Specialist, Certified Vaginal Steam Consultant + Herbalist.

#### About Cassie

Every woman who has ever had a baby has a story to tell...whether good, bad, joyful, overwhelming, exhilarating, peaceful, memorable, etcetera. Most women like to share their experience (or their "birth story"), and sometimes it is easy to think that everyone else's experiences will somehow be our own... but that is not true!
My passion as a childbirth educator and as a doula is to support you and your (growing!) family in YOUR story. I am not here to dictate your story. I am not here to edit your story. I am not here to write your story. I am here to be the ink, the filler, an instrument that you use as you bring your baby into this world and etch another birth into history. I have an ever-abundant fervor for pregnancy, childbirth and postpartum!

##### **Care philosophy**

Free meet and greet
2 or more prenatal visits (as needed)
24/7 availability for phone support
Prenatal, labor/birth, and postpartum resources
Continuous presence and support throughout labor/birth
Immediate postpartum/breastfeeding support for up to 2 hours (as needed)
1+ postpartum visits (as needed)

#### About Kate

At the heart of my doula practice is the belief that birth is not only a profound physical experience but also a radical, political, and deeply hopeful act. Every birth is unique, and every birthing person deserves care that honors their individuality, values, and choices. I am here to provide continuous, compassionate support—wherever and however you choose to give birth.

My approach is rooted in flexibility. Whether you're planning a hospital birth, a home birth, or something in between, I walk with you every step of the way. I’m comfortable in a variety of settings and work seamlessly alongside medical providers, midwives, nurses, and OBs. I believe in collaborative care and see myself as part of a team that centers you—your comfort, your autonomy, your voice.

As your doula, I support your decisions and help you understand your options throughout your pregnancy, labor, and early postpartum period. From evidence-based information about induction and pain relief to navigating unexpected changes in your birth plan, I provide judgment-free guidance. I won’t make decisions for you, but I will ensure you feel informed, heard, and empowered in every moment.

Advocacy is a cornerstone of my practice. That means I help amplify your voice in spaces where it might otherwise be quieted. It means creating space for you to ask questions, push back, change your mind, or stand firm. It means respecting your intuition and experience, even (and especially) when it doesn’t fit the textbook.

Continuity of care matters. Our relationship doesn’t begin and end in the delivery room. From our first prenatal meeting through to postpartum check-ins, I’m a consistent, calming presence. I get to know your story, your fears, your hopes—and I carry them with me as I support you through this life-changing transition.

I am committed to being fully present. I listen deeply. I hold space without judgment. I honor the sacred and the messy. I believe birth is a site of transformation—a moment where the world cracks open and something entirely new begins. That deserves reverence, support, and care that reflects its intensity and power.

Birth is political. It intersects with race, class, gender, ability, and access. I strive to hold an inclusive, anti-oppressive practice. All are welcome here: all genders, all bodies, all families, all birth plans. I continuously work to unlearn bias and practice in a way that supports reproductive justice and birth equity.

Birth is hopeful. It asks us to believe in something unseen, to trust the process of change. As a doula, I hold that hope with you. I believe in your strength, your capacity, and your right to a birth experience that feels safe, supported, and yours.

No matter what your path looks like, I am here to walk it with you.

##### **Care philosophy**

Every birth is a story. I’m here to help you tell yours. My journey toward becoming a doula began with my first pregnancy 15 years ago. I became obsessed with the power and transformation of pregnancy and birth. Four babies of my own later and being honored to be chosen to sit with, support, and witness the births of friends and family along the way, I decided I just wasn’t quite done.

## Doula Services: FAQs

What is a Doula and What Can I Expect from a Birth Doula?

A doula is a trained professional who provides personalized emotional, physical, and informational support during pregnancy, childbirth, and the postpartum period. Unlike medical professionals, doulas do not perform clinical tasks. Instead, they focus on empowering and supporting individuals through their unique birthing journey.

When you hire a birth doula, you can expect continuous, compassionate care tailored to your needs. From pregnancy through labor and delivery, they work closely with you to understand your preferences, alleviate anxieties, and help you make informed decisions. Doulas provide evidence-based information about birthing techniques, pain management options, and medical interventions to guide you in creating a birth experience aligned with your goals.

During labor, a birth doula offers hands-on physical support, such as massage, counterpressure, and guidance on effective birthing positions. They also help you manage discomfort through relaxation and breathing techniques, ensuring a calm and empowering environment. Importantly, they serve as your advocate, helping communicate your birth plan to the medical team so your preferences are understood and respected.

Doulas also support your partner by involving them in the process, offering practical tips for comfort measures, and stepping in so they can rest when needed. After the birth, your doula remains by your side during the postpartum period, assisting with bonding, breastfeeding, or simply ensuring you feel comfortable and cared for.

In essence, a doula’s unwavering support transforms childbirth into a more positive, confident, and empowering experience, allowing you to focus on welcoming your baby with joy.

How do I hire a doula in Fontana, CA?

There are two ways you can hire a doula through Meela in Fontana, CA.

You can browse through the available doulas in Fontana, CA and send the doulas you are interested in a message, introducing yourself and setting up an interview. We recommend interviewing at least three before hiring your top doula.

You can also hire a doula in Fontana, CA in three easy steps:

1. Post a job for free: answer some questions and receive responses from top-rated doulas in Fontana, CA. It takes less than 30 seconds to [post your job](https://www.hellomeela.com/post-a-job/).

2. Get your list of Fontana, CA doulas: in a few hours, you will have a collection of doulas near you who are qualified to meet your needs. You can compare pricing, experience and read reviews. You can chat with each doula to learn more about them.

3. Hire a doula with confidence: schedule virtual or in-person interviews with your top doula choices. Hire your top doula and start getting the care you deserve.

How much does a doula cost in Fontana, CA?

While the cost of a doula can vary based on experience and the services offered, you can expect the average cost of a doula in Fontana, CA to be $1,214, which is the average cost of a doula in the state of California. The cost of a doula in Fontana, CA can range from $997 to $1,431. These costs are based on a representative group of 1,036 doulas surveyed by Meela across the state of California. The average cost of a doula in California is higher than the U.S. national average cost of a doula, which is $971.

When hiring a birth doula in Fontana, CA, you and your doula will discuss and agree upon a fee structure, and your doula will send you itemized invoices through Meela, which you can pay securely through the app.

Will insurance cover the cost of a doula in Fontana, CA?

Some insurance companies will cover all or part of the cost of hiring a doula. Contact your insurance company to find out if they offer any coverage for hiring a doula; not all insurance companies do.

What types of doulas are for hire in Fontana, CA?

You can hire two types of doulas in Fontana, CA: a birth doula and a postpartum doula. The birth doula is during your pregnancy and through the birth of your child. They provide you with emotional, physical and educational support during your pregnancy, your labor and delivery and for the first few hours after your child is born.

A postpartum doula will support you for the first few weeks after the baby is born. They provide in-home assistance, including teaching you how to take care of your newborn, acting as lactation consultant, and can provide some night nanny services.

Can a doula help with a C-section birth?

Yes, a birth doula can provide support during a Cesarean birth and delivery. Doulas are non-medical professionals and do not replace obstetricians, physicians, midwives or nurses. They are an additive member to the team and provide emotional, physical and informational support to the birthing parent, partner and family. It is up to you whether the certified doula can remain in the operating room during the procedure.

What should I expect from a doula?

When you hire a doula in Fontana, CA, your services typically include several in-person prenatal sessions, 24/7 phone and text support, full attendance throughout your labor, and several hours of support immediately following your birth.

A doula in Fontana, CA can help you in any kind of birth. Certified doulas are able to assist parents in creating the ideal “birth environment” and help parents deliver their baby safely. A doula can provide support during the birth whether it occurs in a hospital, a home, or another birth center of some kind. Doulas are there to support you in your desired birth preferences, whether that is a preference for natural, non-medicated, assisted, C-section or anything else.

Certified doulas can also act as a liaison between you and hospital staff regarding your childbirth expectations and preferences. For example, if your preference is to have a non-medicated birth, a doula can help guide you through that process and create a personalized birth plan. A certified doula in Fontana, CA does not replace medical staff (such as your obstetrician and nurses).

When a surgical procedure is required, there are some situations in which a birth doula can also remain in the operating room, although this is not an expected obligation. A certified birth doula can also assist hospital staff to improve the overall birthing experience. A doula in Fontana, CA will typically stay with the parents for several hours after birth to help them with birth recovery.

Can a doula improve my birthing experience?

Evidence suggests that a birth doula in Fontana, CA can help improve parent satisfaction with their baby’s birth and delivery. The involvement of a doula can also lead to shorter deliveries with [less complications](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3647727/).

Recent studies show that parents who have a support person like a doula by their side throughout labor and childbirth are more likely to:

1. Give birth without the need for pain medicine
2. Describe their birth experience in a positive way
3. Give birth without the need for medical intervention, such as C-sections or vacuum delivery
4. Experience a shorter labor

When should I hire a doula?

You can hire a doula early in your pregnancy to allow you time to get to know each other and to begin planning for your later trimesters, labor, birth, and aftercare. It is ideal to hire a doula when you are around 20 weeks along, but you can start looking for one before that.

It is never too late to hire a doula in Fontana, CA; you have right up to your due date to find a doula near you and receive all the benefits of doula support.

What is the difference between a midwife and a doula?

While many people think that a doula and a midwife are the same thing, their educational background and job duties are different. Doulas are non-medical professionals who provide emotional, physical and educational support, while also offering actionable advice and guidance to new parents about how to nurture their baby.

A midwife is a certified nurse-midwife (CNM) or a certified midwife (CM). CNMs are medical professionals that are certified and licensed by the American College of Nurse-Midwives. CMs are also certified, but they do not have nursing backgrounds.

Doulas are not medical professionals; they focus on the emotional support for the parents and the family before, during, and after the birth of the baby. Their job is to provide information, advice, and support to the family not diagnose any medical conditions or provide medical advice.

web.markdown:
[Skip to content](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-providers/#main-content)

[Home](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/)[Providers & Partners](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/)Doula Providers

# Doula Providers

Doulas provide personal support to individuals and families throughout pregnancy and one year postpartum. This includes emotional and physical support provided during pregnancy, labor, birth, and the postpartum period, as well as support for and after miscarriage and abortion.

## Standing Recommendation

As a preventive benefit, doula services require a written recommendation from a physician or other licensed practitioner of the healing arts. To increase access to services, DHCS Medical Director, Karen Mark, MD, issued a [standing recommendation (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Medi-Cal-Doula-Standing-Recommendation.pdf) for the initial set of doula services for any Medi-Cal member who is pregnant or was pregnant within the past year.

The standing recommendation authorizes the following doula services:

- One initial visit.
- Up to eight additional visits that may be provided in any combination of prenatal and postpartum visits.
- Support during labor and delivery (including labor and delivery resulting in a stillbirth), abortion or miscarriage.
- Up to two extended three-hour postpartum visits after the end of a pregnancy.

If a Medi-Cal member has used all of the visits for the standing recommendation, the member would need an additional recommendation from a physician or other licensed practitioner of the healing arts acting to receive up to nine additional postpartum visits. This recommendation can be noted in the member’s medical record by the recommending licensed provider, or a member can ask a licensed provider to complete the [Medi-Cal Doula Services Recommendation: Additional Postpartum Visits form  (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Medi-Cal-Doula-Recommendation-postpartum.pdf) and give the signed form to the doula. The standing recommendation from DHCS cannot be used for additional postpartum visits. For more information regarding recommendations, please see the General Frequently Asked Questions below.

## How to Become a Medi-Cal Doula Provider

Doulas who meet the qualifications listed in the [Medi-Cal Provider Manual: Doula Services (this is a pdf file)](https://mcweb.apps.prd.cammis.medi-cal.ca.gov/assets/0075B242-F893-41DB-A418-4129A274E46C/doula.pdf?access_token=6UyVkRRfByXTZEWIh8j8QaYylPyP5ULO) may enroll through the [Provider Application and Validation for Enrollment (PAVE) portal](https://pave.dhcs.ca.gov/sso/login.do) to submit their Medi-Cal application. For additional information on the requirements to become a Doula Medi-Cal Provider, please see the
[Medi-Cal Doula Provider Enrollment Checklist  (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Medi-Cal-Doula-Provider-Enrollment-Checklist.pdf),

Doulas interested serving Medi-Cal managed care plan (MCP) members will also need to enter into contracts with the member’s Medi-Cal MCP plan. Doulas must enter contracts with each Medi-Cal MCP in their geographical area in order to serve Medi-Cal members who receive services through each Medi-Cal MCP and to receive reimbursement for doula services provided to Medi-Cal MCP members.

## Doula Directory

The [Doula Directory](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-directory/) lists Medi-Cal enrolled doula providers by county who opted to be included in the directory. It includes their specialties, language, ethnicity, contact information, and other information that doulas shared with DHCS for the directory. DHCS updates the directory once each month.

Medi-Cal members with fee-for-service may use the directory to find a doula. Medi-Cal members in a managed care plan (MCP) should contact Member Services for their MCP to assist them in securing doula services. MCP point of contact for members can be found in the [Medi-Cal Managed Care Plan Member Contact List (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Member-2025-Doula-Services-MCP-Doula-Directory-2025.pdf).

The Department has enrolled 1,576 individual providers as of June 5, 2026.

## Managed Care Delivery System

To assist doulas with contracting and onboarding with managed care plans (MCP), submitting claims and receiving payment, please refer to the information in the Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – Managed Care Plans. For assistance with contacts at a specific MCP, please email [DoulaBenefit@dhcs.ca.gov](mailto:DoulaBenefit@dhcs.ca.gov).

## Resources

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ):

- [Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – General FAQs](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-services-frequently-asked-questions-doula-providers-general-faq/)
- [Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – Enrolling as a Doula](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-services-frequently-asked-questions/)
- [Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – Managed Care Plans](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-services-faq/)
- [Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – Reimbursement](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-services-frequently-asked-questions-doula-providers-reimbursement-faq/)
- [Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – Hospitals](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-services-frequently-asked-questions-doula-providers-hospitals/)
- [Frequently Asked Questions for Doulas – Dispute Resolution Process](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/doula-services-frequently-asked-questions-faq-doula-dispute/)

### Enrolling as a Doula

- [Provider Application and Validation for Enrollment (PAVE) portal](https://pave.dhcs.ca.gov/sso/login.do)
- [Medi-Cal Doula Provider Enrollment Checklist  (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Medi-Cal-Doula-Provider-Enrollment-Checklist.pdf)
- [Doula Demonstration 1: PAVE Demo for Doulas](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SnYSNw2ZFuY)
- [Doula Demonstration 2 : Basic Functions in PAVE for Doula Providers](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bGCwNETTj-U&feature=youtu.be)

### Billing Assistance

- [Billing Tips for Doula Providers (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/Billing-Tips-Doulas.pdf) for fee-for-service members
- [Medi-Cal FFS Doula Billing Codes Chart](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/providers-partners/medi-cal-ffs-doula-billing-codes-chart/)
- [Doulas – Training as Medi-Cal Providers](https://mcweb.apps.prd.cammis.medi-cal.ca.gov/references/doulas) (enrollment and billing information)

### Policy Documents

- [Medi-Cal Provider Manual: Doula Services (this is a pdf file)](https://mcweb.apps.prd.cammis.medi-cal.ca.gov/assets/0075B242-F893-41DB-A418-4129A274E46C/doula.pdf?access_token=6UyVkRRfByXTZEWIh8j8QaYylPyP5ULO)
- [All Facilities Letter (AFL) 25-13](https://www.cdph.ca.gov/Programs/CHCQ/LCP/Pages/AFL-25-13.aspx)

- [MCP Member Resources – Websites (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/MCP-Contact-List-Resources-Websites.pdf)

### Fact Sheet

- [Services for Pregnant People and New Parents (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/BD-Perinatal-Services-Offered-Flyer.pdf) (February 2025)
- [Spanish Version (this is a pdf file)](https://www.dhcs.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/BD-Perinatal-Services-Offered-Flyer-Spanish.pdf)

### Other Resources

Write a review

5.0

October 10, 2025

ANGELINA, we have already made it clear to you. You have already went to yelp. We have reviewed your reviews., you go around making bad reviews because at a snap of a finger, one time visit we could not make due to an emergency induction for another mom. Out of 300 births, you are the only person who has ever tried to hurt my business the way you have. I personally feel that you have to heal in your postpartum that’s a priority to me versus my feelings at this point I have parted ways with you for more than one reason this is a public platform and I will not disclose any of your personal information. I did an absolutely fantastic job as doula , I did not ask you to write a review on this website, you made flaws claims and accusations about contracts. I have never made anyone sign. You made false claims about me not being communicative with you about a postpartum visit. You made false claims in regards to my professional professionalism. I helped watch your sweet baby boy while you were able to have a successful delivery. I have treated your family with absolute respect. But one thing I will not tolerate about my business as false accusation.. I will not move forward with any type of care in the future, and we have parted ways.

\- Justina B.

3.0

October 10, 2025

I was asked to do this review a bit early, which was off putting to me since services were not completed yet. However I will leave my honest review for services provided up til this point in time. Justina is a very sweet, personable, knowledgeable doula. She is available 24/7 by phone/text, however her in person availability is very limited. I find this to be a big issue especially when I stressed my area I would need most help is postpartum and I am now almost 4 weeks postpartum and still have yet to have a visit. Even prior to birth she did want to do one prenatal visit with my OBGYN, however that was rescheduled twice due to her availability and the next appt I never even made it to since my water broke early. I had my baby early, she was due October 18 but born Sep 14. Baby didn't get to leave nicu to come home until Sep 23 so it's been about 2 weeks of her being home and still no postpartum visits or scheduling of them without me initiating the conversation. I do know Justina had a vacation which I believe ended Sep 24 so I knew she wasn't available until after that, but getting a text from her asking for a review not anything about scheduling postpartum visits was very unsettling. I actually had even reached out to her Tuesday Sep 30 asking if we could schedule visits and she said she wasn't available til Monday to discuss so I said no worries. Then Friday she sent this yelp review text so imagine my surprise how a review took precedent over scheduling postpartum visits. We did schedule a visit for Wednesday 11am which was rescheduled on Wednesday morning to Friday. I fear that she is over booked and not every client gets the same attention as others due to this. This is not my first rodeo with a doula, however it is my first experience with such a popular one that doesn't have time to book my appts. She mentioned she has 7 moms in October, I was also due in October originally, so if I'd like my 1 hr postpartum visits scheduled I will need to reach out to her so she can pencil me in. I find this very unprofessional. I feel as if I'm booking a baby sitter at that point. My previous experience my doulas were reaching out to me to schedule postpartum visits, not the other way around. I booked and signed intake form with her back in July so to me scheduling should not be an issue with so much notice. Overall I feel she is a great birth doula, although I can't fully speak on that as my birth was C-section, but postpartum not so much. Again I have not yet had a postpartum visit but I stressed this was the most important to me as I don't have a village to help me, if I would've known it would be nearly impossible to get some of her time for those I would've booked someone else.

\- Angelina C.

What support resources are available for caregivers of patients receiving care from Labor Coach (Doula)s in Fontana, CA?

Your Labor Coach (Doula)'s office in Fontana, CA may be able to provide information about local support groups for caregivers, or there are several online communities you can access. How can patients effectively advocate for themselves during appointments with Labor Coach (Doula)s in Fontana, CA?

It is important to remember that you are a partner with your Labor Coach (Doula) as you find the right treatment plan for you. You have a voice in your healthcare decisions, and it is important to communicate your goals and preferences clearly to get the highest quality of care.

web.title: Find a DONA Certified Doula
web.description: Parents, if you are looking for a birth or postpartum doula, this is the place to search your search. Use our database to find a DONA certified doula.
web.markdown:
[Home](https://dona.org/)/ [Explore Doula Support](https://dona.org/find-a-dona-certified-doula/#)/ [Find a DONA Certified Doula](https://dona.org/find-a-dona-certified-doula/)

## Find a DONA Certified Doula

## Benefits of Working with a DONA Certified Doula

DONA-certified doulas are trained to provide evidence-based, ethical, client-centered support. Certification must be earned and maintained through continued education and evaluation, ensuring professionalism and compassionate care throughout the birth or postpartum experience.

## How to Choose a DONA Certified Doula

Choosing the right doula is personal and can influence your birth or postpartum journey. Clarifying your needs and values can help guide your search.

## Decide what type of doula you need.

Birth doulas are trained to provide physical, emotional, and informational support throughout labor and delivery. The doula helps with comfort measures like positioning, counter pressure, and breathing techniques, offers emotional support to create a nurturing space for the birthing parent and their partner, and serves as a bridge of communication to help families make informed decisions and advocate for the very best care.

Postpartum doulas are trained to understand what new babies – and new parents – truly need. The doula helps with soothing techniques, offers lactation or bottle feeding support, and explains normal newborn behavior and postpartum recovery expectations.

Examples:

- Birth (CD/AdvCD)
- Postpartum (PCD/AdvPCD)

### Define Your Priorities

- Do I want someone with specific experience (home birth, hospital, first-time parent)?
- Is cultural, spiritual or language alignment important to me?
- What type of support do I expect most: emotional, informational, advocacy or hands-on?

## Search for Doulas

Use DONA International’s directory to find certified doulas by location and specialty, then interview candidates to ensure the right fit.

## Trusted Training & Professional Standards

DONA International doulas complete extensive training and follow professional guidelines designed to support families safely and respectfully.

- Rigorous certification process requiring hands-on experience and recertification every 3 years
- Certification includes a Code of Ethics & Standards of Practice for client-centered care
- Continuing education encourages up-to-date, research-based support
- Peer support network that fosters mentorship, collaboration and referrals

[Discover Our Resources](https://dona.org/find-a-dona-certified-doula/#)

## Get your Free Guide to Hiring a Doula

How do you hire a doula? What questions should you ask a doula before you choose them as part of your birth team? Learn more about best practices in the doula field.

# Online Doula Training: August 3 – 30, 2026

**Register by June 15th for 15% off!** Training begins **Monday, August 3, 2026**. SMC Full Circle Doula Birth Companion Online Training is a four-week course for dual certification for the labor and postpartum doula.

[View Training details](https://smcdoulas.com/courses/online-doula-training-august-3-30-2026/#learndash-course-content)

## About the Training

**EARLY BIRD REGISTRATION NOW OPEN – REGISTER BY JUNE 15 FOR A 15% DISCOUNT WHEN YOU REGISTER!**

- Includes:

- SMC Doula Training Manual
- Orientation Packet
- SMC Full Circle Doula Provisional Certificate\*
- Certification Packet\*
- One-Year Provisional Membership to the SMC Doula Network, for ongoing support toward full certification.\*
- Added to the Find a Doula Directory\*
- Membership in the SMC Full Circle Doula Birth Companions Private Facebook Group, for fun networking!\*

\*Based on successful completion of the training.

SMC Full Circle Doula Birth Companion Doula [Online training](https://smcdoulas.com/smc-online-doula-training-and-certification/) is a four-week course for dual certification for the labor and postpartum doula. SMC Full Circle Doula Birth Companion Training was created in 2002 to train doulas in the legacy of the 20th-century African American midwife who demonstrated traditional and spiritual perinatal care.

The training is designed to build capacity in the Black community, increase the number of doulas of color, and teach the midwifery model of care.

## Training Schedule

**Week 1:** Orientation and Full Circle Doula Care, August 3 – 9, 2026

**Week 2**: Pregnancy Care and Nutrition, August 10 – 16, 2026

**Week 3:** Labor and Delivery, August 17 – 23, 2026

**Week 4:** Postpartum Care & Entrepreneurship, August 24 – 30, 2026

**Live Zoom Class with Instructors:  Each Week on Wednesday from 11:00 am PT– 2:30 pm PT.**

Virtual Graduation: September 2, 2026 –  11:00am – 12:30pm PT

## Frequently Asked Questions

**All registered learners attending the training receive:**

- Exclusive SMC Doula Training Manual

- Weekly Live Zoom Session during the course where you can interact with instructors

- Resource List

- Provisional Doula Certificate\*

- Certification Packet\*

- One-Year Provisional Membership to the SMC Doula Network, for ongoing support toward full certification.\*

- Added to the Find a Doula Directory\*

- Membership in the SMC Full Circle Doula Birth Companions Private Facebook Group, for fun networking!\*
\*Based on successful completion of the training.


**What topics are covered?**

**Unique topics include and extend beyond:**

- Honoring the Legacy of the African American Midwife

- African Brith Traditions

- Postpartum Care & Belly Binding

- Public Health in Maternal and Infant Health

- Improving Birth Outcomes

- Supporting Fatherhood/Partners and Family

- Doula History and the African American Doulas

- SMC Doula Scope of Care (SOC)

- Pregnancy Support & Nutrition

- Medical Terminology

- Creating a Business

- Working with the Birth Team

**Who can attend the training?**

The only requirements to enroll in the training are that you (1) are 18 years of age or above, (2) have a high school diploma or GED (3) are interested in supporting women and families before, during, and after birth.

_SMC Doulas uses women-centered language and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion (creed), gender, gender expression, age, national origin (ancestry), disability, marital status, sexual orientation, or military status in any of its activities or operations._

**How should I prepare?**

To prepare for the training, please read the required reading before the first day of your course.

**What is the required reading?**

- SMC Full Circle Doula Philosophy, an abstract by Shafia M. Monroe

[Download PDF — 10-pages](https://shafiamonroe.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/08/Full-Circle-Doula-Training-Philosophy.pdf)

- Listen to Me Good, Life Story of an Alabama Midwife, by Margaret Charles Smith and Linda Janet Holmes
- Mothering the Mother, Postpartum Traditions, Recipes and Healing, by Shafia Monroe.  You can order [**here**](https://www.amazon.com/Mothering-Mother-American-Postpartum-Traditions-ebook/dp/B0DSBHTDWL "https://www.amazon.com/Mothering-Mother-American-Postpartum-Traditions-ebook/dp/B0DSBHTDWL").

**Who are the instructors?**

Instructors are SMC Certified Trainers who are veteran doulas and midwives. You can learn more **[here](https://smcdoulas.com/meet-the-trainers/).**

**When will I receive my manual?**

Your training manual will be shipped to the shipping address provided on your registration form.

**Please allow 14 business days to receive your training manual** if shipped within the United States.

Deliveries outside of the U.S. may be subject to customs and delivery restrictions.

Please allow an additional 7 business days for export deliveries.

**What is the value of an SMC Doula Network Provisional Doula membership?**

Membership is essential for accessing your training materials and manual after the course ends, staying in touch with your cohort and others through the SMC Doulas Network, receiving support with your certification, accessing additional educational materials to further your professional development as a doula, and having the public find you through doula directory. Membership allows you to grow the doula industry and receive discounts on conferences and some webinars. Learn more [here](https://smcdoulas.com/signup/provisional-doula-membership/).

**What payment options are available?**

You can pay over time or make a one-time payment via debit, or credit card.

- Single Payment of $1523

- Two Weekly Installments of $761.50

- Three Weekly Installments of $507.67

- Four Weekly Installments of $380.75

**Please Note:** _Registration fees must be paid at least 30 days in advance of the first day of training._
